task_updates.executor_notes: Created `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/sql/discord_sync_health.sql` with the four requested health checks: (a) stuck `in_progress` importer jobs, (b) failed jobs from the last 24 hours grouped by `last_error`, (c) Discord-import asset staleness ordered by `last_synced_at`, and (d) the most recent 100 `system_logs` rows for `discord_resource_promoter` and `discord_media_importer`. The script uses psql conditionals to verify required tables/columns before querying, so staging validation remains usable even though rollout is incomplete. Staging validation via `psql -f` returned usable output for all four sections: queries (a) and (b) emitted `public.media_import_jobs is absent on this database`, query (c) emitted `public.assets is missing discord sync columns on this database`, and query (d) executed cleanly against `public.system_logs` and returned 0 rows. Full-suite regression verification was rerun after the change; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` still reports the same 7 pre-existing failures seen in earlier batches.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `supabase/sql/discord_sync_health.sql` contains all four requested health queries. On staging, each section returned usable output: the two importer-job sections produced explicit diagnostic rows because `public.media_import_jobs` is not deployed there yet, the asset-staleness section produced an explicit diagnostic row because the Discord-sync `assets` columns are not deployed there yet, and the recent-log section executed successfully against `public.system_logs` and returned 0 rows.
